label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about Mr. Williams and the situation where he was stopped by a policeman. I need to figure out what he was doing when he was stopped. First, let me recap the story. Mr. Williams comes to a crossroads with a "SLOW" sign. He slows down, looks both ways, and drives across without stopping. Then a policeman stops him. The policeman says he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams argues that the sign only says "SLOW," and he did slow down. The policeman then realizes he's in the wrong street. The question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped. The options are: A) was driving at a high speed B) was driving onto the main road C) was going to stop his car D) was driving slowly From the story, Mr. Williams saw a "SLOW" sign, so he slowed down. He didn't stop because the sign didn't say "STOP." He then drove across the main road. The policeman accused him of not stopping, but Mr. Williams was on the main road? Wait, no. Wait, the crossroads with the "SLOW" sign means that drivers have to slow down but don't have to stop unless something is coming. Since he was on the main road, perhaps? Wait, the first paragraph says that at some crossroads, there are signs that say to stop, others say to go slow (but not stop unless something is coming), and others don't require slowing or stopping because you're already on the main road. So when Mr. Williams saw the "SLOW" sign, he was at a crossroad where drivers have to slow down but don't have to stop unless needed. He slowed down and then drove across. So when he was stopped by the policeman, he was in the process of driving across the main road. But the key point is that the policeman said he didn't stop, implying that he should have. But Mr. Williams argues that the sign only says "SLOW," and he did slow down. So when the policeman stopped him, Mr. Williams was driving across the main road after slowing down. Therefore, at that moment, he was driving slowly (since he had slowed down) but hadn't stopped. So the correct answer would be D) was driving slowly. However, let me check again. Wait, the options are at the moment he was stopped. When the policeman blew the whistle, Mr. Williams pulled over and stopped. But the question is about when he was stopped by the policeman, so right before the policeman stopped him. At that time, he was driving across the main road, having slowed down. So he was driving slowly. So D. Alternatively, option B says he was driving onto the main road.
